definition: In 2022, Japan's energy balance-of-payments swing — current account from 20 trillion yen surplus to near balance as energy imports surged from 17T to 28T yen — was the primary JPY weakness driver. Real FX flow from energy importers ($8-12bn/month) exceeded speculative carry flow ($3-5bn/month). Demonstrates that physical flow dynamics can overwhelm rate differentials and challenges the dominant rate-differential narrative.
evidence: Japan energy import surge from 17T to 28T yen annualized
Energy importer real FX flow $8-12bn/month vs speculative carry $3-5bn/month
Current account swing from 20T yen surplus to near balance
Yen safe-haven failure in 2022 (USD/JPY 115 to 152 during risk-off) was rate-environment-contingent
Japan 2022: ¥11 trillion surge in energy import bill
Real USD buying flows 2-3x larger than speculative carry positioning
Corporate hedger order flow operates on different timescales and price sensitivity than speculative flows
open_questions: Has the energy BoP channel permanently impaired the yen's safe-haven property?
Will nuclear restarts reduce Japan's energy import bill enough to materially affect JPY?
How Japan's nuclear restart and renewable buildout will alter this channel over time
